title: caltha sinogracilis f. rubriflora
intro: Caltha sinogracilis f. rubriflora is a plant species in the buttercup family, known for its vibrant red flowers and medicinal properties. The plant's roots, rhizomes, and leaves are used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Caltha sinogracilis f. rubriflora is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help to reduce pain and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat respiratory problems and skin conditions.
